narrative: Jindal Stainless is the overall FY2025 benchmark leader, ranking 1st in Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3, physical-output intensity and Scope 1+2 turnover intensity. Its 1.85 tCO2e/tcs physical intensity is the strongest steel-process signal in the set, supported by scrap-based EAF production, renewable-energy sourcing, biofuel replacement, waste-heat recovery, hot charging, auxiliary power reduction, liquid chrome use and VFD installations. SAIL is the best performer on Scope 3 turnover intensity at 4.90e-06 tCO2e/₹ and ranks 2nd in absolute Scope 3, but its Scope 2 and physical-output intensity worsen in FY2025. Tata is the laggard across four of six metrics: Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3 and physical-output intensity. Its 3.2 tCO2e/tcs is 73% higher than Jindal’s, and Scope 3 is 615% higher in absolute terms. The strategic implication for Tata is clear: the Net Zero 2045 pathway depends on accelerating blast-furnace decarbonisation, renewable/captive low-carbon power, scrap/EAF transition and tighter Scope 3 category management.
